response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a full-stack web and systems developer whose career has centered on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — not C++. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2000–2001 and a bachelor thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. He has no demonstrated fixed-income, capital-markets, or investment-banking domain expertise (a brief stint at RIDE Capital involved agile practices, not financial instruments). SQL experience is listed as a skill but is neither prominent nor tied to SQL Server or Oracle, and his primary database work is with MongoDB. None of the desirable extras (Summit, Kafka/MQ, Python) are evidenced, and there is no Windows-platform focus anywhere in the record.
strengths: Long career with consistent delivery across diverse technical domains, demonstrating adaptability and breadth
Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps exposure (GitLab, Docker, Artifactory) satisfies one of the optional desirable skills
Public speaking, workshops, and open-source projects suggest solid written and verbal communication abilities
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — roughly six months in 2000–2001 and a thesis project — against a mandatory requirement of 5–10 years
No fixed-income, capital-markets, or investment-banking domain knowledge anywhere in the employment history
Primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang with MongoDB; no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency required by the role

key_factors.factor: No capital-markets or fixed-income domain exposure
Strong communication and delivery track record
